requirements.statement: The action contract shall expose overridable hooks before and after validation and after run.
use Jido.Action shall generate metadata accessors plus JSON-serializable action metadata from compile-time configuration.
The action contract shall validate declared input and output fields while preserving unspecified fields for composition.
The tool bridge shall coerce incoming params and run actions through Jido.Exec, returning JSON-safe results or errors.
Actions shall expose tool definitions with JSON-schema parameter descriptions for AI callers.
Jido.Exec.Chain shall compose actions from modules, tuples, and action-option pairs while passing intermediate params through the chain.
Jido.Exec.Chain shall stop cleanly when an interrupt check requests early exit and return the partial result accumulated so far.
Async chain execution shall run in an unlinked task and optionally route through an instance-specific task supervisor.
Async await and cancel operations shall clear monitor and result messages for both current and legacy async references.
Compensation execution shall not leak monitor or result messages after timeout or crash handling.
Jido.Exec shall route work to the global task supervisor by default and to instance-specific task supervisors when the jido option is provided.
Jido.Exec shall run action compensation after terminal failures, preserve original failure context, and report compensation timeout or crash details.
Jido.Exec shall retry recoverable execution failures while suppressing retries for validation and configuration errors or explicit no-retry hints.
Jido.Exec shall resolve timeout, retry, and backoff defaults from runtime config when explicit opts are absent and fall back safely when config values are invalid.
Jido.Exec shall run timeout-bound actions under supervised tasks, return structured timeout or task-exit errors, and clean up timed-out child processes.
Jido.Exec.Closure shall capture action, context, and execution opts into reusable sync and async callables over later params.
Execution entrypoints shall accept only supported ok and error tuple shapes, preserve directives, and validate declared output schemas on successful results.
Jido.Exec shall run actions synchronously or asynchronously and enforce async ownership on await and cancel operations.
Jido.Exec shall support full, minimal, and silent telemetry modes for runtime execution.
Execution telemetry and log payloads shall redact sensitive fields, truncate large data, and stay inspect-safe for nested structs.
Jido.Instruction.validate_allowed_actions shall reject instructions whose action is not in the allowed set.
Jido.Instruction.new and new! shall require an atom action and apply defaults for id, params, context, and opts.
Jido.Instruction.normalize and normalize! shall accept modules, tuples, structs, and flat lists, then merge shared context and opts into each instruction.
The package shall provide a validated core action behavior that lets applications define structured actions with schemas and metadata.
The package shall expose AI-compatible tool conversion for actions.
The package shall provide dependency-ordered action chaining with interruption and async supervision support.
The package shall isolate instance-specific execution supervisors and keep async or compensation mailbox state from leaking into callers.
The package shall provide retries, timeout supervision, compensation, and runtime-default execution policy controls.
The package shall provide direct, async, and closure-based execution entrypoints for running actions.
The package shall expose telemetry and logging that redacts sensitive data and stays safe for large nested payloads.
The package shall let applications normalize instruction inputs into Jido.Instruction values.
The package shall let applications compose dependency-aware plans from normalized instructions.
The package shall provide shared runtime errors, utility helpers, and default execution supervision for Jido.Action.
The package shall provide schema adapters and a JSON Schema bridge for action validation and tool projection.
Jido.Plan shall build named steps from action modules and instruction shorthands while carrying plan context into each normalized instruction.
Jido.Plan normalization shall reject undefined or cyclic dependencies before execution.
Jido.Plan.execution_phases shall derive topological execution phases from the normalized dependency graph.
Jido.Action.Error shall expose concrete exception structs and helper constructors for validation, execution, configuration, timeout, and internal failures.
The package runtime shall provide a default task supervisor for Jido.Exec when no instance-specific supervisor is selected.
Jido.Action.Util shall provide conditional logging, name validation, and result normalization helpers for action runtime code.
Jido.Action.Schema shall recognize empty, NimbleOptions, Zoi, and JSON Schema map inputs, expose known keys, and validate config schemas without creating unsafe atoms.
Jido.Action.Schema.JsonSchemaBridge shall translate the supported JSON Schema subset into Zoi schemas, preserve legacy param conversion behavior, and fall back cleanly on unsupported constructs.
Jido.Action.Schema shall emit JSON Schema for supported schema inputs and apply strict additionalProperties controls when requested.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that gives local operating guidance for agents working inside the .spec workspace, including when to update ADRs.
The repository shall include a .spec/decisions/README.md that explains when durable ADRs belong in the workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/README.md that explains purpose, layout, and workflow.
When planning and verification run, the workspace shall generate .spec/state.json containing indexed subjects, indexed decisions, and verification state.

then: the hook can transform the result
the result stays a single {:ok, map()} tuple
when: the hook receives a successful action result

given: an action declares an output schema with required fields
the action returns those required fields plus extra data
then: the extra data is preserved in the result

given: a list mixes action modules, tuples, and instruction structs
a shared context is provided to normalization
then: each item becomes a Jido.Instruction struct
the shared context is merged into each instruction

given: a normalization input contains a nested list
then: normalization returns an error instead of silently flattening input

given: a plan references a dependency that is not defined as a step
a plan has multiple independent steps followed by a merge step
then: the independent steps share a phase
the merge step is scheduled after them

meta.summary: Core action behavior and lifecycle contract for Jido.Action modules.
AI tool conversion and execution bridge for Jido.Action modules.
Chain composition, interruption, and async supervision for Jido.Exec.Chain.
Instance supervision, async ownership, and mailbox hygiene for Jido.Exec.
Retry, timeout, compensation, and runtime-default execution policy for Jido.Exec.
Direct, async, and closure-based execution entrypoints for Jido.Exec.
Telemetry mode selection and payload sanitization for Jido.Exec.
Construction, normalization, and allowlist checks for Jido.Instruction values.
Package contract covering action definition, schema handling, execution runtime, AI tool conversion, and workflow composition.
Builder, dependency validation, and execution phase contract for Jido.Plan.
Shared errors, utility helpers, and global execution supervision for Jido.Action.
Schema type detection, JSON Schema projection, and JSON Schema bridge behavior.
Canonical workspace contract for authored specs and generated Spec Led state.
